creator: voland@lflat.org
text:
On Tue, Nov 07, 2000 at 10:58:41AM -0800, Nick Hibma wrote:
> Could you be more descriptive of the circumstances? Does it occur at the
> end of printing, during switching off of the printer, at the start of
> printing.

	Yep, I had no time for more details while submitting.

	It's happening while starting printing. There were definitely some
detachments just before panic - this I can see from kernel debugging
messages. In fact, this is the only detail I forgot to mention.

> Did you try the patch sent to you by Ian Dowse <iedowse@maths.tcd.ie>?
> It will probably solve your problem.

	I wasn't able yet. Gonna try updating my -CURRENT.

-- 
    /Voland			Vadim Belman
				E-mail: voland@lflat.org
